properties.evidence: In general, alternative risk factor markers have had mixed results in clinical trials. Even the most positive studies have not shown a dramatic improvement in prediction of future CVD risk compared to traditional Framingham risk scoring.

In general, alternative risk factor markers have had mixed results in clinical trials. Even the most positive studies have not shown a dramatic improvement in prediction of future CVD risk compared to traditional Framingham risk scoring.

In an accompanying editorial, lipid expert Dr. Scott Grundy concluded that “apolipoproteins are of limited value in reclassifying individuals among arbitrary risk categories, i.e., low-risk, intermediate-risk, and high-risk”.
